# Houston Intercontinental Airport (IAH/KIAH) — Historical June Precipitation & Climate Normals

## Station Information
- **Station**: Houston Bush Intercontinental Airport (KIAH)
- **Identifiers**: ICAO: KIAH, IATA: IAH, COOP/ACIS: USW00012960, WBAN 12960, WMO 72243
- **Period of Record**: 1969–present (57 years)

---

## Monthly Climate Normals

### 1991–2020 Normal (Official NOAA 30-year baseline)
- **Mean June precipitation: 6.00 inches (152.3 mm)**
- **Median: 4.48 inches (113.8 mm)**
- Standard deviation: 4.72 inches — reflecting Houston's highly variable, storm-driven June rainfall
- The large mean–median gap indicates a **strongly right-skewed distribution**: a few very wet Junes (tropical storms, persistent convection) pull the mean well above what a "typical" June delivers.

### Full Period of Record (1969–2025)
- Mean: 5.49 in (139.5 mm) | Median: 4.46 in (113.3 mm)

---

## Weekly Precipitation Breakdown (1991–2020 Normals)

| Period | Mean (in) | Mean (mm) | Median (in) | Median (mm) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Week 1 (Jun 1–7)** | 1.20 | 30.4 | 0.46 | 11.7 |
| **Week 2 (Jun 8–14)** | 1.26 | 32.0 | 0.62 | 15.9 |
| **Week 3 (Jun 15–21)** | 1.64 | 41.6 | 1.18 | 29.8 |
| **Week 4 (Jun 22–28)** | 1.55 | 39.4 | 1.11 | 28.2 |
| **Jun 29–30** | 0.36 | 9.0 | 0.04 | 0.9 |

**Key pattern**: The second half of June (15th–30th) averages **~3.55 inches**, roughly **44% more** than the first half (~2.46 inches). This reflects the typical Gulf Coast pattern of intensifying convective activity as SSTs and atmospheric moisture rise through June.

---

## Rainy Days (1991–2020)
- **Mean days with measurable precipitation (≥0.01 in): 10.0 days**
- Mean days with ≥0.10 in: 6.6 days
- Range: 1 day (2009) to 20 days (2004)
- **Probability of rain on any given June day: ~33%**
- Late June (22nd–30th) has the highest rain frequency at 44–60% per day

---

## Extreme Records

### Wettest Junes at IAH
| Rank | Year | Total (in) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2001 | 19.21 | Tropical Storm Allison |
| 2 | 2004 | 18.33 | |
| 3 | 1989 | 16.28 | |
| 4 | 1973 | 13.46 | |
| 5 | 2016 | 13.12 | |

### Driest Junes at IAH
| Rank | Year | Total (in) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2005 | 0.08 |
| 2 | 2022 | 0.13 |
| 3 | 1970 | 0.26 |
| 4 | 2009 | 0.27 |
| 5 | 1974 | 0.59 |

### Wettest Single Days in June (since 1991)
| Date | Precip (in) | Event |
|---|---|---|
| 2001-06-08 | 8.13 | Tropical Storm Allison |
| 2001-06-05 | 5.33 | TS Allison precursor |
| 2006-06-19 | 4.43 | |
| 2016-06-01 | 3.94 | |
| 2021-06-02 | 3.77 | |

---

## Decadal Trends

| Decade | Mean June (in) |
|---|---|
| 1970–1979 | 4.47 |
| 1980–1989 | 5.83 |
| 1990–1999 | 5.46 |
| 2000–2009 | 6.23 |
| 2010–2019 | 6.15 |
| 2020–2025 | 5.04 |

The 2000s and 2010s were the wettest decades on record for June at IAH, though 2020–2025 data (small sample) suggests a possible reversion toward the longer-term mean.

---

## Percentile Distribution (1991–2025)

| Percentile | Value (in) |
|---|---|
| 10th | 0.80 |
| 25th | 2.72 |
| 50th (median) | 4.56 |
| 75th | 6.68 |
| 90th | 8.83 |

---

## Data Quality Note
NOAA ACIS (official station gauge data) was used as the primary authoritative source. ERA5 reanalysis (Open-Meteo) was used for cross-referencing but systematically underestimates extreme convective events (e.g., TS Allison 2001: NOAA 19.21 in vs. ERA5 ~7.6 in). The NOAA ACIS figures should be considered definitive.

key_facts: IAH (KIAH, USW00012960) 1991–2020 climate normal for June precipitation is 6.00 inches (152.3 mm), with a median of 4.48 inches — indicating a strongly right-skewed distribution driven by occasional extreme rainfall events.
June precipitation at IAH is significantly wetter in the second half of the month: Weeks 3–4 (Jun 15–28) average ~3.19 in combined vs. ~2.46 in for Weeks 1–2 (Jun 1–14), a roughly 30% increase.
The average number of days with measurable precipitation in June is 10.0 (1991–2020), with a probability of rain on any given day of ~33%, rising to 44–60% in late June.
The wettest June on record at IAH was 2001 (19.21 inches), driven by Tropical Storm Allison which produced 8.13 inches on June 8 alone — the single wettest June day on record.
The driest June on record was 2005 with only 0.08 inches, demonstrating the enormous year-to-year variability (standard deviation of 4.72 inches).
The 2000s and 2010s were the wettest decades for June precipitation at IAH (6.23 and 6.15 inches respectively), above the full-record mean of 5.49 inches.
The full period of record (1969–2025) June mean is 5.49 inches (139.5 mm) with a median of 4.46 inches.

Week 1 (Jun 1–7) averages 1.20 in with median 0.46 in; Week 2 (Jun 8–14) averages 1.26 in with median 0.62 in; Week 3 (Jun 15–21) averages 1.64 in with median 1.18 in; Week 4 (Jun 22–28) averages 1.55 in with median 1.11 in.
